thresholds._note: The Codex-SOL failure-mode edge rows for the production-path gate: multi-reference (two ids, one capability), same-name-cross-domain (a name valid in two domains, disambiguated by the capability), multi-turn/facet-carry (a terse follow-up inherits the prior facet), an intended entity-only abstain, and an alignment-miss (oblique reference). multi_reference / same_name_cross_domain / multi_turn are gated on exact-capability; entity_only is gated on the 100% abstain invariant; alignment_miss is report-only (routing may legitimately abstain when masking is incomplete). Test data — domain literals allowed.

queries.query: Compare the settlement status for REL-00042 and REL-00099.
queries.note: Two references of the same entity type (two rel ids) with ONE capability — both spans mask, routing keys on 'settlement status' and lands on settlement_status once (not two competing routes).

queries.query: Show the holdings for both the Whitman Family Office and the Calderon Trust.
queries.note: Two names, one wealth capability — must route to holdings, not split into noise.

queries.query: What is the settlement risk on the Whitman Family Office?
queries.note: Whitman (REL-00042) is a relationship visible in BOTH wealth and servicing. The capability 'settlement risk' disambiguates to the asset-servicing capability — the shared name must not drag the route to a wealth capability.

queries.query: Show portfolio concentration risk for the Whitman Family Office.
queries.note: Same shared name, but 'portfolio concentration risk' is a wealth capability — disambiguates the OTHER way. The pair (with sncd_settlement_risk_shared_name) proves the capability, not the name, decides the domain.

messages.content: REL-00042 has 2 pending and 1 failed settlement.

queries.note: Facet-carry (bug-233 / spec Piece 3): the terse 'and for REL-00099?' inherits the prior turn's settlement facet — the window carries an explicit grounded id, so routing does not margin-abstain into a clarify — and routes to settlement_status again.

messages.content: Pull up the Whitman Family Office holdings.
Whitman holds 14 positions across 3 asset classes.
and the performance on that portfolio?

queries.note: Anaphora + new facet: 'that portfolio' resolves to the last focal entity (Whitman); the NEW facet 'performance' must re-route to performance, not stay on holdings.
Entity with NO capability action — after masking the residual is near-empty, so routing must abstain into a clarify rather than guess a capability from the name alone.

queries.query: Can you show me REL-00188 please?
queries.note: Entity + stopwords/request-verbs only — masking the id leaves 'can you show me please', which the near-empty policy treats as no action → abstain/clarify, not a blind route.

queries.query: What's the risk tolerance for the family office we discussed earlier?
queries.note: Report-only. An oblique reference ('the family office we discussed') has no alignable verbatim id/name, so masking is INCOMPLETE and relaxation is forfeited — routing may still key on 'risk tolerance' (capability-first) or abstain. This row surfaces the alignment-miss behaviour for the operator; it is not a hard gate.
